crazy_prediction.text: {"name":"The race is red-flagged and suspended due to lightning, with a delay of 30+ minutes before resuming","reasoning":"AccuWeather forecasts an 88% chance of rain and 53% chance of thunderstorms at race time. Miami law requires sporting events to be suspended for nearby lightning, with a mandatory 30-minute wait after the last thunderclap before resuming. Combined with the new FIA wet-weather rules (banned boost button, no rear wing DRS), this is a perfect storm for an unprecedented lightning-induced race suspension. [sports.yahoo.com](https://sports.yahoo.com/articles/anyone-beat-mercedes-miami-2026-130924860.html)"}

flop.text: {"name":"Verstappen","reasoning":"Red Bull is in a deep slump, Verstappen is openly contemplating quitting F1, and his race engineer Lambiase has announced he's leaving for McLaren. Red Bull has been hit-and-miss with upgrades for years. With only 90 minutes of practice before sprint qualifying on a sprint weekend, if their Miami upgrade doesn't work - which has happened repeatedly - Verstappen could have a miserable weekend. His head clearly isn't fully in it. [foxsports.com.au](https://www.foxsports.com.au/motorsport/formula-one/f1-2026-miami-grand-prix-preview)"}

good_surprise.text: {"name":"McLaren","reasoning":"McLaren is bringing an entirely new MCL40 to Miami with completely overhauled aerodynamics (front/rear brake ducts, bodywork, floor, rear wing). Andrea Stella confirmed this is the big upgrade they've been building toward. Piastri was already fighting for the win in Japan, and Miami historically suits McLaren - remember the 2024 Miami upgrade that transformed their season. With Piastri having won here in 2025 and a chaotic wet forecast favoring McLaren's strategy team, they could legitimately challenge Mercedes for victory. [planetf1.com](https://www.planetf1.com/news/martin-brundle-ferrari-upgrades-mercedes-mclaren-f1-2026-fight)"}

crazy_prediction.text: {"name":"The race is red-flagged for lightning or thunder in the vicinity of the circuit","reasoning":"Miami law requires all sporting events to be suspended immediately if lightning or thunder is detected nearby, with a mandatory 30-minute wait after the last thunderclap before any activity resumes. Sunday's forecast specifically calls for thunderstorms and heavy rain arriving around race time. The 2026 cars have never raced in wet conditions, and with the pre-emptive FIA ban on the boost button and DRS in the wet, the combination of untested machinery and a lightning-enforced red flag could create unprecedented scenes. This is a uniquely Miami risk that no other circuit on the calendar faces to the same regulatory degree."}

flop.text: {"name":"Red Bull Racing","reasoning":"Red Bull has a well-documented history of hit-and-miss upgrade packages in recent years, often finding themselves down developmental dead-ends. They are expected to bring a major upgrade to Miami, but if it fails to deliver — as several of their past mid-season updates have — they risk falling even further behind Mercedes, McLaren, and Ferrari. Verstappen's open quit threats and the looming departure of race engineer Gianpiero Lambiase point to a team under internal strain. Wet conditions could further expose any correlation issues with their new parts."}

good_surprise.text: {"name":"Haas","reasoning":"The five-week break has given all teams time for major upgrades, and Haas could arrive with an effective package under the radar. As the only American team on the grid, the Miami home race provides extra motivation. With thunderstorms forecast and the new 2026 cars largely untested in wet conditions, the chaotic environment could allow a midfield team like Haas to punch above its weight — especially with the boost button banned in the wet, potentially levelling the power unit playing field."}

flop.text: {"name":"Red Bull Racing","reasoning":"Red Bull are bringing updates, but the baseline has looked too far away: Verstappen was only eighth in Japan, Red Bull have been described as well adrift of Mercedes in the constructors’ picture, and Hadjar himself has cautioned that the Miami upgrade will not fix everything. good_surprise.text: {"name":"McLaren","reasoning":"Mercedes arrive as the clear benchmark after winning the opening races, but Miami looks like the first real reset point: McLaren have been targeting this weekend for a major upgrade push, they already showed stronger race pace with Piastri second in Japan, and the Sprint format plus only one practice session increases the chance that a well-correlated aero package jumps them into victory contention immediately. Miami has also been a historically good upgrade venue for McLaren, so I’m backing them to beat expectations and seriously threaten Mercedes rather than merely collect solid points."}
